stories.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
stories.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

stories.title: Iran fires more missiles at Israel and rejects Trump's talk as 'fake news' for markets
stories.description: Israeli health officials said Iranian missiles struck four sites across Israel Tuesday, including central Tel Aviv, injuring at least six people. Iranian authorities also said a gas supply line in southwest Iran was struck overnight.

stories.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
stories.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

stories.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
stories.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

stories.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
stories.description: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: 'It's been bittersweet': The Iranian-Iraqi cafe drawing crowds to Hackney
stories.description: Logma, a newly opened Iranian-Iraqi cafe in Hackney, has gone viral for its sandwiches.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: 'We do not have a single war on Iran: We have an Israeli war and an American war'
stories.description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and International Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. Mr. Lucas warns there has been no breakthrough whatsoever in diplomacy: An improvised and reactive process shaped by mar...

stories.title: Israel and US interests diverge on possible negociation for end of war
stories.description: While U.S. President Donald Trump projects optimism that an end to the war with Iran can be negociated soon, Israel seems determined to keep up the fight. The possibility of the US ending or even withdrawing from the conflict poses a significant risk to Israel's own goals.

stories.title: Israel's occupation of South Lebanon 'way to pressure Lebanese government' over Hezbollah
stories.description: The Israeli Defence Forces are setting a military zone in south Lebanon of about 30 kilometres between the border and the Litani river. The military also said it had destroyed a bridge across the river overnight, which it says was used by Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbo...

stories.title: Trump's supposed talks with Iran: What do we know?
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday walked back his own deadline for escalating attacks on Iran, citing "productive" talks with Tehran – but Iranian officials deny those talks took place. So what is really happening? Here is a look at the facts and speculation.

stories.title: Middle East war weighs heavy on Iranian population
stories.description: Getting a sense of the real death toll and the psychological toll of the war in the Middle East is a real challenge. According to estimates, at least 1,500 Iranians have died since the start of the US-Israeli military campaign. We spoke to a sociologist on how it is effecting civ...

stories.title: US 'shifts' towards diplomacy with Iran ahead of midterms
stories.description: Hopes rose that the Iran war could begin winding down as U.S. President Donald Trump said that his government was holding productive talks with Tehran. Although this was denied by Iran, it is a further sign that the US is turning towards diplomacy to end a conflict that could dra...

stories.title: Israeli forces to control south Lebanon up to Litani river
stories.description: Israel said on March 24 that its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani River, around 30 kilometres (20 miles) from the border, as deadly strikes pounded the country. The day before, Israel's finance minister had said Israel should annex the region. This co...

stories.title: What do we know about the negotiations between the US and Iran?
stories.description: It's day 25 of the US and Israel's war with Iran, and US President Donald Trump claims that "very good" talks are underway to end combat. He said that because of this, the US will hold off on striking Iranian power plants. But Iranian officials have contradicted this - downplayin...

stories.title: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation
stories.description: Cubans, who have historically received preferential treatment from the US, have not been spared by President Donald Trump's war on immigration. The US has ended family reunification and legal immigration from Cuba by adding the island to its travel ban. It has also suspended temp...

stories.title: Nigeria's Dangote refinery increases exports as Middle East war shakes global markets
stories.description: Nigeria's Dangote Petroleum Refinery has stepped up gasoline exports across Africa as disruptions to energy flows due to the Iran conflict squeeze traditional ​fuel ‌supply routes, curbing the cheap imports that long dominated West African ⁠markets. As the Strait of Hormuz remain...

stories.title: Moment rescuers find man alive under the rubble in Tehran
stories.description: Rescuers have pulled a man alive from the rubble after US-Israeli strikes hit a residential area in Tehran.

stories.title: Keir Starmer’s policy on the Iran war is a recipe for catastrophe
stories.description: The British government has learned no lessons from the disaster that the US-UK invasion of Iraq was.

stories.title: QatarEnergy declares force majeure on some LNG contracts due to Iran war
stories.description: The move comes amid production disruptions linked to the US-Israeli war on Iran, which have affected Qatar.

stories.title: Is the US talking to Iran’s Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and who is he?
stories.description: Iranian leaders, including Ghalibaf, have denied that talks with the US are under way.

stories.title: Pakistan ‘ready to host US-Iran talks’: Can latest peace push work?
stories.description: Pakistan, Turkiye and Egypt are trying to broker talks. Analysts caution the chasm between the US and Iran remains wide.

stories.title: What are Iran’s cluster munitions that are penetrating Israeli defences?
stories.description: Cluster bombs disperse munitions over a wide area and are particularly dangerous for civilians, analysts say.

stories.title: Qatar’s foreign affairs ministry stresses need for regional coexistence
stories.description: Qatar’s foreign affairs ministry spokesman said that coexistence in the region is essential despite Iranian attacks.

stories.title: Adviser questions US justification for war on Iran
stories.description: Brian Finucane says US intelligence failed to substantiate claims of an ‘imminent threat’ from Iran

stories.title: The US should end the war asap
stories.description: The best way to manage the fallout of the US-Israel war on Iran is to end it now.

stories.title: Missing Iranian uranium hinders Trump’s victory declaration
stories.description: Former NATO official William Alberque says 440 kg of missing Iranian uranium is blocking a US victory claim.

stories.title: ’Terrified’ Israeli residents recount Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Video shows the aftermath of an Iranian missile attack on the Israeli town of Nesher.

stories.title: Pakistan stands ‘ready, honoured’ to host US-Iran talks, says PM Shehbaz
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on Tuesday that Pakistan stood “ready and honoured” to host “meaningful and conclusive talks” between the US and Iran. “Pakistan welcomes and fully supports ongoing efforts to pursue dialogue to end the war in the Middle East, in the interest of...

stories.title: North Korea’s Kim vows ‘irreversible’ nuclear status, warns Seoul of ‘merciless’ response
stories.description: North Korea will never give up nuclear weapons, leader Kim Jong Un said, indicating that it will soon designate South Korea the “most hostile state”, according to a state media report on Tuesday. Kim also told the country’s legislature in a policy address on Monday that the Unite...

stories.title: Australia, European Union agree sweeping new trade pact 8 years in the works
stories.description: The European Union (EU) and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on Tuesday, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. EU chief Ursula von der Leyen’s visit to Australia come...

stories.title: US journalists sue to protect Voice of America from political control
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Journalists at the US government-funded broadcaster Voice of America (VOA) have filed a lawsuit accusing the Trump administration of undermining the outlet’s independence and turning it into a tool for political messaging. The complaint, filed on Monday in the US Dist...

stories.title: US deploys ICE agents at 14 airports
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The US immigration agency at the centre of a firestorm over heavy-handed enforcement tactics began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigra...

stories.title: Pakistan in the limelight as US, Iran ‘open backchannel’
stories.description: • Efforts alongside Egypt and Turkiye seen as key to pausing threatened US strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure• Administration sources say diplomatic efforts saw Islamabad leveraging its position as ‘net regional stabiliser’• PM speaks to Pezeshkian, Dar calls Araghchi; White...

stories.title: Trump’s Iran claims roil markets as Tehran denies talks
stories.description: https://www.dawn.com/news/1984751

stories.title: No ceasefire in Gaza as Israel continues to terrorise Palestinians
stories.description: • UN experts decry ‘medicide’ following attacks on healthcare workers• One-year-old child tortured with cigarettes to force father’s ‘confession’• Investigators decide not to question officers who killed West Bank family AS global attention shifts toward the US-Israel war with Ir...

stories.title: Indonesia says no to $1bn fee for Trump’s ‘Board of Peace’ seat
stories.description: JAKARTA: Indonesia’s president, under fire at home for signing up to US counterpart Donald Trump’s so-called “Board of Peace”, has insisted his country would not pay the $1 billion joining fee for permanent membership. Jakarta had only committed peacekeeping troops for the initia...

stories.title: Ex-BBC employees take broadcaster to court over Gaza coverage
stories.description: LONDON: A UK employment tribunal has heard claims that BBC imposed editorial restrictions on its journalists and misled the public during coverage of Israel’s assault on Gaza in October2023, according to media reports. The case, brought by five former BBC Arabic journalists Amer ...

stories.title: World gave Israel ‘licence to torture Palestinians’: UN expert
stories.description: GENEVA: The world has given Israel “a licence to torture Palestinians”, a UN expert said on Monday, with life in the occupied territories “a continuum of physical and mental suffering”. “Torture has effectively become state policy” in Israel, said Francesca Albanese, the UN’s spe...

stories.title: Trump backs down on strikes on Iran’s power network, claims US and Tehran holding talks
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday he had given orders to postpone any military strikes against Iranian power plants for five days, hours ahead of a deadline that threatened further escalation in the conflict now in its fourth week. Trump claimed in a post on his Truth Soci...

stories.title: Israeli minister calls for annexation of southern Lebanon, extension of border
stories.description: Israel should extend its border with Lebanon up to the Litani River deep inside the country’s south, Israel’s finance minister said ​on Monday as Israeli troops bombed bridges and destroyed homes in the area in an escalating military assault. The comments by Finance Minister ‌Bez...

stories.title: Mideast war threatens energy crisis worse than 1970s oil shocks, warns IEA chief
stories.description: The world faces an energy crisis worse than both 1970s oil shocks combined if the Middle East war drags on, the head of the International Energy Agency warned on Monday, as Israel launched fresh strikes on Tehran and threatened weeks more fighting. As the war grinds into its four...

stories.title: In phone call with Pezeshkian, PM stresses ‘urgent need’ for collective efforts for de-escalation in Mideast
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif held a telephone call with Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian on Monday, during which he stressed the “urgent need” for collective efforts for de-escalation in the Middle East and a return to dialogue and diplomacy. The phone call took place as the ong...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: PM Modi speaks to Trump, says India supports de-escalation and restoration of peace at the earliest
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump hailed “very good” talks with an Iranian official; Tehran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, reportedly involved in talks, said "no negotiations" were underway

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
